raw_title: The Effects of AI Attribution, Source Priming, and Story Topic Polarization on News Credibility
title_en: The Effects of AI Attribution, Source Priming, and Story Topic Polarization on News Credibility
title_zh: The Effects of AI Attribution, Source Priming, and Story Topic Polarization on News Credibility
plain_summary_en: This paper studies The Effects of AI Attribution, Source Priming, and Story Topic Polarization on News Credibility. In plain terms, it asks what is happening, why it matters for public communication, and what evidence the authors use. The likely focus is Credibility, Source credibility, Attribution, Priming (agriculture). The abstract suggests a mixed methods approach, which makes the work useful for understanding how communication changes in everyday life.
plain_summary_zh: This paper studies The Effects of AI Attribution, Source Priming, and Story Topic Polarization on News Credibility. In plain terms, it asks what is happening, why it matters for public communication, and what evidence the authors use. The likely focus is Credibility, Source credibility, Attribution, Priming (agriculture). The abstract suggests a mixed methods approach, which makes the work useful for understanding how communication changes in everyday life.
research_direction_en: The paper points to an active research direction around Credibility, Source credibility, Attribution, Priming (agriculture), especially how institutions, platforms, and audiences shape communication outcomes.
research_direction_zh: The paper points to an active research direction around Credibility, Source credibility, Attribution, Priming (agriculture), especially how institutions, platforms, and audiences shape communication outcomes.
